patch.value:
## Problem
`pm comments <id> --body "..."` fails with `Unknown option --body`. gh/glab muscle memory makes --body the reflexive flag for comment text; every fresh agent session pays ~2 wasted tool calls + a few hundred tokens rediscovering --add. Observed in a real session 2026-07-10 (vanilla @unbrained/pm-cli 2026.7.10).

## Proposed fix
1. Accept `--body <text>` as a hidden alias for `--add <text>` on `pm comments` (and mirror for `--edit` replacement text if cheap).
2. Extend the unknown-option error path to suggest the nearest valid flag — the unknown-COMMAND suggester exists; unknown-OPTION suggestions are the gap (ranking defect on the command side is pm-g543 / GH-490, related edge).

## Scope guard
Alias only — no behavior change to --add; help output must not grow (hidden alias) per token-efficiency goals.

## Source
https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/498
GH-498: pm comments rejects --body — accept it as an alias for --add and hint on unknown options

Upstream GH-498 (filed 2026-07-10). Agents trained on gh/glab reach for 'pm comments <id> --body ...' and hit 'Unknown option --body', costing a failed call plus a --help round-trip per fresh session on one of the highest-frequency write paths.

pm comments <id> --body <text> behaves exactly like --add <text> (hidden alias, help stays lean); unknown-option errors on comments suggest the nearest valid flag (e.g. --body -> --add); contract/completion/docs sites updated per the new-flag wiring checklist; regression test covers alias and suggestion; GH-498 closed with a comment linking the fix

author: maintainer-agent
message: Set risk/confidence per active-item metadata baseline

author: maintainer-agent
message: Cross-link: unknown-option suggester half is owned by pm-gdi7; this item's primary deliverable is the comments --body alias
